History of having been treated with a potassium-wasting diuretic such as thiazide (chlorothiazide, chlorthalidone, hydrochlorothiazide, indapamide, metolazone) and loop (bumetanide, ethacrynic acid, furosemide, torsemide) diuretics.
History of having been treated with a tricyclic antidepressant medication.
Pain improves or worsens with changes in body position.
A family history (in a first- or second-degree relative) of unusually severe, recurrent, or opportunistic infection.
A family history (in a first- or second-degree relative) of an immune deficiency.

synonyms.val: Familiy history of primary immunodeficiency disorders (PID)
Family history of an inborn error of imunity (IEI)

definition.val: A family history (in a first- or second-degree relative) of an autoimmune disease.

nodes.lbl: Family history of death due to infection

definition.val: A family history of death of a first- or second-degree relative caused by infection.
A family history (in a first- or second-degree relative) of a lymphoma.

comments: A family history of lymphoma may indicate inherited cancer predisposition syndromes, including DNA repair defects, and inborn errors of immunity predisposing to lymphoproliferative disease such as X-linked lymphoproliferative syndrome and ITK deficiency.

definition.val: A family history (in a first- or second-degree relative) of a leukaemia.

comments: A family history of leukaemia may indicate inherited bone marrow failure syndromes, DNA repair defects, or inborn errors of immunity predisposing to malignancy, including GATA2 deficiency, SAMD9/SAMD9L disorders, and Fanconi anaemia.

definition.val: A maternal history of recurrent miscarriage, defined as two or more pregnancy losses.

synonyms.val: Maternal history of recurrent spontaneous abortion

definition.val: Death of a sibling that remains unexplained despite investigation, which may indicate an undiagnosed disease in the family.

A reproductive relationship between two individuals who are related by blood as second cousins or closer. In a medical and genetic context, consanguinity increases the coefficient of inbreeding, raising the probability that an offspring will inherit identical alleles from a common ancestor (homozygosity by descent). This significantly increases the risk for autosomal recessive disorders, such as inborn errors of immunity, metabolic diseases, and rare neurodevelopmental syndromes. A history of consanguinity serves as a vital clinical marker for directing diagnostic investigations toward recessive genetic etiologies, especially in the presence of rare phenotypes or unexplained family medical history.
Past medical history of surgical breast removal.
Aspiration or inhalation of lipid-containing products.

comments: Exogenous lipoid pneumonia is caused by aspiration or inhalation of lipid-containing products. In the past, the most common agents included mineral oil-based laxatives and nasal decongestants. Other causes include inhalation of petroleum jelly used as a lubricant, e.g., tracheostomy care. The incidence of exogenous lipoid pneumonia is growing exponentially in all age groups secondary to the phenomenon termed vaping. In the latter case, consider the term Electronic cigarette exposure histor (HP:0430032).

definition.val: Refers to pain that is worse when the affected individual lies down (flat).
History of treatment with an anthracycline chemotherapy agent.

nodes.lbl: Lack of sufficient daily physical activity

definition.val: Physical activity levels less than those required for optimal health and prevention of premature death.
A history of high intake of spciy foods with ingrediaents such as capsaicin, pepper, chili, ginger, garlic, onion, fenugreek, and turmeric.
A history of being at high altitude in situations such hiking, mountain climbing, travel to mountains, or air travel.
A past medical history of recently being hospitalized.

comments: This may imply a nosocomial infection risk.

definition.val: Cessation of palpitations can be induced by Valsava maneuver.

comments: In patients who can terminate their palpitations with a Valsalva maneuver, this is thought to indicate possibly a supraventricular tachycardia.

definition.val: Neuropathic pain is triggered by lesions to the somatosensory nervous system that alter its structure and function so that pain occurs spontaneously and responses to noxious and innocuous stimuli are pathologically amplified.

comments: Neuropathic pain can be characterized by pain that is triggered when clothes touch the skin, spontaneous burning that feels like boiling water, bursts of pins and needles(paresthesias) in the feet upon walking , a continuous crushing pain after an amputation as if your phantom foot is being squeezed, or a band of searing pain around the body at the level at whichsensation was lost after a spinal cord injury.

definition.val: A history of repeated exposure to cold water, as may result, for instance, from surfing, diving, kayaking, or swimming.
Tinnitus (sound heard by a subjhect when no corresponding external sound is present) that is made worse by swallowing.

comments: During swallowing, the tensor veli palatini (TVP) muscle is activated to assist in elevating the palate. Due to this dual innervation by CNV, the tensor tympani (TT) muscle can be activated and possibly cause tinnitus in some patients.

definition.val: Applies to a sign or symptom that is improved or made more bearable by treatment with nitroglycerin.
Chest pain can be induced by palpation of the chest, which may be an indication of costochondritis.
Discomfort in the chest described as pressure, heaviness, or tightness.

nodes.lbl: Chest pain described as ripping or tearing

definition.val: Chest pain described as a tearing or ripping sensation.

comments: The pain of aortic dissection may be described as tearing or ripping and does not improve with nitroglycerin.

definition.val: Applies to a sign or symptom that is improved or made more bearable by bending (flexing) a joint.
Applies to a finding that is situated or occurring around the thorax (chest).
Applies to a finding that is situated or occurring in or on the penis.
History of multiple previous births.
History of exposure to aquatic animals.

comments: Mycobacterium marinum is an opportunistic pathogen inducing infection in fresh and marine water fish. Human infections with Mycobacterium marinum have been associated with the exposure of damaged skin to polluted water from fish pools or contacting objects contaminated with infected fish. Fish mycobacteriosis is clinically manifested and characterized in man by purple painless nodules, liable to develop into superficial crusting ulceration with scar formation.

nodes.lbl: Exacerbation of eyelid lesion by crying

definition.val: Applies to an eyelid lesion such as hemangiomas or varices that swells upon crying.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated in the upper eyelid.
This term refers to pain that is triggered by extending (straightening) the leg.

comments: This feature may suggest spinal problems such as spinal epidural abscess.

definition.val: Refers to a sign or symptom that is improved or made more bearable by anesthetic block of a nerve.

nodes.lbl: History of urethral procedure or catheterization

definition.val: A past medical history of a procedure or catheterization involving the urethra.
Applied to a sign or symptom that is present during pregnancy and becomes worse following birth.
History of having taken retinoids.
History of having been treated with propylthiouracil.

History of spending a substantial amount of time outdoors with occupation or hobbies related to the outdoors, including hunting, gardening, farming, agriculture, horticulture, miners, working in wet fields.
Past medical history of surgical replacement of the cornea with corneal tissue from a donor.
A past medical history of wearing contact lenses.
Precipitous labor is extremely rapid labor and delivery, usually defined as expulsion of the fetus within less than 3 hours of commencement of regular contractions.
Maternal history of a pregnancy with two fetuses in the uterus.
Refers to a phenotypic abnormality that mainly affect large joints.
Refers to a phenotypic abnormality that affects a single joint.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated in (or predominant in) the elbow.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated in (or predominant in) the heel.
Ingestion of large amounts of licorice.

comments: Licorice is a popular sweetener found in many soft drinks, food products, snacks and herbal medicines. Licorice toxicity is characterized by the symptom triad of hypertension, hypokalemia, and metabolic alkalosis, so-called pseudohyperaldosteronism. The active ingredient in licorice is glycyrrhizin, which is converted to 3beta-monoglucuronyl-18beta-glycyrrhetinic acid (3MGA) and 18beta-glycyrrhetinic acid (GA) in the gut. These active metabolites inhibit the 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type II enzyme and prevent the conversion of cortisol to cortisone. Unlike cortisone, cortisol binds to the mineralocorticoid receptor. This cortisol excess leads to increased potassium and proton excretion as well as to sodium and water retention, with increased systemic resistance. The triad of symptoms typical of classic hyperaldosteronism (Conn syndrome) results from the aldosterone-like effect: hypokalemia, hypertension, and metabolic alkalosis.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality that is situated in (or predominant in) the shoulder.

Intrauterine devices (IUDs) are used for contraception. Rarely pregnancy occurs despite the presence of an IUD. Such pregnancies are associated with an increased risk of spontaneous abortion and preterm delivery due to infection, and a higher risk of maternal septic complications.
The mother of a fetus or index patient has a history of folate deficiency during the corresponding pregnancy.
The mother of a fetus or index patient has a history of abnormally low zinc levels during the corresponding pregnancy.

comments: Maternal zinc deficiency may increase the risk low birth weight (LBW) and small for gestational age (SGA) infants.

definition.val: Intrauterine devices (IUD) are commonly used for contraception. IUD complications are rare, but include perforation, pelvic infection, expulsion, missing strings, and difficult IUD removal.

nodes.lbl: History of recent central venous catheter

definition.val: A past medical history of a recent central venous catheter (CVC). A CVC is an indwelling device inserted into a large, central vein (most commonly the internal jugular, subclavian, or femoral) and advanced until the terminal lumen resides within the inferior vena cava, superior vena cava, or right atrium. CVCs can be a necessary producre for critically ill patients with indications such as total parenteral nutrition administration, dialysis, plasmapheresis, medication administration, and hemodynamic monitoring, and to facilitate further complex interventions such as transvenous pacemaker placement. Complications include arrhythmia, central vein stenosis, and sepsis.

nodes.lbl: History of total parenteral nutrition dependence

definition.val: A past medical history of a recent depence on total parenteral nutrition (TPN), which refers to intravenously administered nutrition being the only source of nutrition the patient is receiving.
This refers to testicular pain that is lessened or made more bearable by elevation of the testicle.

comments: Prehn sign, the relief of pain with the elevation of the testis, may be elicited in patients with epididymitis, although this is not a reliable finding.

nodes.lbl: Ear pain exacerbated by manipulating tragus or pinna

definition.val: Applies to pain of the ear that is worsened, aggravated, or exacerbated by touching or moving the tragus or pinna.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated in the occiput (back part of the head).
History of having received gastric bypass surgery. This is a procedure that reduces the functional volume of the stomach and is used to treat morbid obesity.

Applies to a sign or symptom that is provoked or brought about by exposure to volatile organic compounds (VOCs), which comprise various organic chemicals which are released as gases from different liquids or solids.

comments: VOCs are present in different household, industrial or commercial and products, but their accumulation in air and water has primarily gained attention. Among VOCs, trichloroethylene and vinyl chloride are the most toxic and carcinogenic compounds.

definition.val: A medical history of having undergone radiation therapy, which is a treatment that uses ionizing radiation for purposes such as the control the growth of malignant cells in cancer treatment.
History of exposure to respirable crystalline silica (SiO2).

comments: Silicosis is a well known consequence of silica dust exposure, and exposure has also been associated with the risk of lung cancer, pulmonary tuberculosis, and other airway diseases.

definition.val: A past medical history of having had a surgical procedure to remove the adrenal glands.

nodes.lbl: Recent history of caustic substance ingestion

definition.val: Medical history of a recent ingestion of a caustic substance, i.e., a highly acidic or alkaline chemicals that can cause severe burns to the mouth and digestive tract when swallowed.
A past surgical history of the implanation of an artificial heart valve.

nodes.lbl: History of recent plant thorn skin puncture

definition.val: A history of a plant skin puncture preceding the current presenting condition by hours, days, or weeks.
A type of fracture that occurs in the trabecular cancellous bone just beneath the subchondral bone plate without disruption of the articular surface.

nodes.lbl: History of recent iodated contrast medium administration

definition.val: Medical history of recent iodinated or radioiodine contrast use.

comments: Iodinated contrast media commonly used in various radiologic examinations and interventional procedures results in massive iodide exposure to the thyroid gland. Whereas people with normal thyroids usually experience no ill effects, those with pre-existing thyroid disease may experience thyrotoxicosis (often referred to as thyroid storm in this context), which, if unrecognized, could lead to serious, potentially life-threatening consequences, including atrial arrhythmias, heart failure, pulmonary arterial hypertension, cerebrovascular and pulmonary embolism, and cardiomyopathy.

definition.val: Difficulty inserting a nasogastric tube (NGT), a flexible plastic tube that is placed through the nose and esophagus into the stomach and used to administer drugs or for feeding. There are certain circumstances that may make NGT insertion difficult. They are esophageal narrowing, comatose or intubated patients, and patients who have sustained severe facial or skull injuries.
A medical history in a male of not having been circumcized.

comments: Neonatal male circumcision is a common surgical procedure.

definition.val: A medical history of use of facial makeup.

comments: Facial makeup use may be associated with canaliculitis, which refers to inflammation of the lacrimal canaliculus.

definition.val: A type of displaced fracture in which there has been a rotation of the distal fracture fragment in relation to the proximal portion.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated on the skin in areas where bones are close to the surface (bony prominences).
A past medical history of wearing tight shoes, toe-box shoes, or high heel shoes.

comments: This may be a risk factor for Morton neuroma.

definition.val: Medical history of recently eating wild mushrooms.
Refers to symptomatology that begins in the proximal limbs and subsequently affects the distal limbs.
Exposure to continuous light at night, including night-shift work or a nocturnal lifestyle.

nodes.lbl: Menstruating female between menarche and menopause

definition.val: Past medical history of a woman who is menstruating, i.e., not premenstrual or post-menopause.
A past medical history that indicates that the patient had a surgical procedure involving the abdomen.
Onset of signs or symptoms of disease during the period of a woman's life shortly before the occurrence of the menopause.
Onset of signs or symptoms of disease during the period of a woman's life after the occurrence of the menopause.

Loss of a substantial amount of blood during delivery (some definitions stipulate 1000 ml or more), typically associated with manifestations such as low blood pressure, a rapid heart rate, dizziness, light-headedness, fatigue, and weakness.

Applies to a sign or symptom that is improved or made more bearable by treatment with an agent that reduces gastric acidity such as proton pump inhibitors, histamine receptor blockers,omeprazole, esomeprazole, dexlansoprazole, pantoprazole, lansoprazole, famotidine, or ranitidine.
Applies to a sign or symptom that is improved or made more bearable by ingestion of riboflavin (Vitamin B2).
Applies to an abnormality whose distribution and appearance are located at regions of the male face covered by a beard or mustache.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated in (or predominant in) the trunk of the body. The trunk is the main part of a person's body, not including the head, legs, or arms.

nodes.lbl: Occupational exposure to raw nonsynthetic textiles

definition.val: A history of exposure by inhalation of airborne particles generated during the processing of raw, nonsynthetic textiles, particularly cotton. Classically, exposure to cotton dust during the spinning and manufacturing process causes byssinosis. However, exposure to jute, flax, and hemp fibers has also been implicated in its development.
The concentration of vitamin B12 in the mother's blood during pregnancy is below the lower limit of normal.
A medical history of having been exposed to livestock (animals such as cattle and sheep which are kept on a farm ).
Applies to an abnormality of the skin in which multiple lesions occur at the same time.
Refers to a skin lseion that has a snake-like (serpentine) shape.

comments: Lesions of granuloma inguinale are said to have a serpentine appearance.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality of the skin that is located in or surrounding eccrine sweat glands.

comments: Eccrine sweat glands are simple, coiled, tubular glands present throughout the body, most numerously on the soles of the feet. Eccrine sweat glands serve a thermoregulatory function via evaporative heat loss. When the internal temperature of the body rises, sweat glands release water to the skin surface. There, it quickly evaporates, subsequently cooling the skin and blood beneath.; this is the most effective means of thermoregulation in humans. Eccrine sweat glands also participate in ion and nitrogenous waste excretion.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality of the skin whose boundary to the surrounding normal skin is not clearly defined.
Medical history of a recent bite or scratch injury due to a cat.

nodes.lbl: History of recent stay in area with inadequate sewage sanitation

definition.val: A history of having been in an area with inadequate sewage sanitation in the recent past.
A history of an injury to the temporal region of the skull that occurred recently (several hours, days, or weeks before the current presenting complaint).
Applied to a sign orr symptom that is worsed by administration of opiate medications.

comments: Opiate drugs increase pain in narcotic bowel syndrome.

definition.val: A history of exposure to fragrances found in cosmetic products.
A past medical history of having been treated with tight bandages, compression sleeve, tight casts or splints, immobilizers, or similar items such as overly tight clothes, watch bands, or handcuffs.
Applies to a sign or symptom that is induced by repeatedly performing overhead motions, such as painting a ceiling or working on objects located above the head, or some types of athletics.

comments: This trigger can apply to rotator cuff syndrome.

definition.val: Pain that is perceived to extend or spread out from one area of the body to another.
Applied to a sign or symptom that is more severe during mensutruation.

comments: Lipoprotein(a) [Lp(a)] is a macromolecular complex in human plasma which represents a quantitative genetic trait with a heritability of 0.7 or higher. High concentrations of Lp(a) are associated with atherothrombotic disease i.e. myocardial infarction, stroke, peripheral vascular disease and childhood thromboembolism. The covalent Lp(a) complex is assembled in plasma from low-density lipoprotein (LDL) and apolipoprotein(a), a plasminogen-related glycoprotein.

nodes.lbl: New medication added in recent weeks

definition.val: Medical history of starting a new medication recently.

comments: Certain conditions can arise from reactions to a vast number of different medications, so this is a newness finding rather than one for any particular drug.

definition.val: Pain at night.
Applies to a sign or symptom that is provoked or brought about by trauma to the eye.
A history of ingestion of undercooked pork. This is particularly relevant in areas with a high prevalence of cysticercosis.
A history of exposed to rodents (mice, rats, prarie dogs) within several weeks of the current presenting complaint.

nodes.lbl: Recent history of moisture in external ear canal

definition.val: A recent history of being exposed to moisture in the external ear canal.

comments: Swimmer's ear may occur when excess moisture in the ear canal allows bacteria or fungi to grow more easily. Risk factors include swimming and use of ear buds while exercising (and sweating).

nodes.lbl: History of recent trauma to external ear canal

definition.val: Medical history of trauma to the external ear canal, including cotton-swab use, in recent weeks.
Exaggerated posterior vertebral notches.
History of having taken olanzapine.

comments: Olanzapine exposure has been associated with acute pancreatitis.

definition.val: The mother of a fetus or index patient has a history of regular exposure to one or more cats, e.g., in the household.

comments: This exposure may confer a risk for toxoplasmosis in the fetus.

definition.val: Applied to a sign or symptom that is aggravated by (or made more painful by) pronation. Pronation of the hand involves the rotation of the forearm and wrist so that the palm of the hand faces downwards or towards the back (posteriorly).

nodes.lbl: History of wearing tightly fitted clothing

definition.val: History of wearing tight clothing such as compression shorts, sports bras, bike shorts, wet suits, spandex, veils, and tight hats.

comments: This may be a risk factor for tinea.

definition.val: Localization to or predominance in the temporal cortex of the brain.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated in (or predominant in) the head and neck region.
Applies to an abnormality that is highly similar on the left and right sides of the body.
Applies to an abnormality that is situated in extensor surface of an extremity.

comments: For instance, in the upper extremity, this would refer to a lesion of the skin over the triceps as opposed to the biceps.

nodes.lbl: Recent history of sedation by propofol infusion

definition.val: A history of exposure to anesthesia by propofanol infusion recently before the presenting complaint.

comments: Propofol is commonly used in anaesthesia and in intensive care where its favourable pharmacokinetics and rapidly reversible sedation make it an ideal drug for short-term sedation. However, propofol has been associated with a number of serious adverse effects such as metabolic acidosis, cardiac asystole, myocardial failure, rhabdomyolysis, and death.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality whose distribution and appearance are solitary and localized to a part of the skin, a tissue or an organ that differs from the surrounding healthy tissue.
Applied to a sign or symptom that is more severe following a meal.

A past medical history of recently having retracted the penile foreskin for a prolonged period of time. Paraphimosis commonly occurs iatrogenically, when the foreskin is retracted for cleaning, placement of a urinary catheter, a procedure such as a cystoscopy, or for penile examination. Failure to return the retracted foreskin over the glans promptly after the initial retraction can lead to paraphimosis.

comments: This can be a risk factor for paraphimosis, a urologic emergency that occurs in uncircumcised males when the foreskin becomes trapped behind the corona of the glans penis.

definition.val: History of having been treateed with a sodium glucose transport inhibitors such as dapagliflozin, empagliflozin, canagliflozin, sglt-i, SGLT2, bexagliflozin, ertugliflozin, sotagliflozin, jardiance, invokana, forxiga, brenzavvy, steglatro, inpefa.

comments: May be a risk factor for SGLT-2i induced diabetic ketoacidosis.

definition.val: A history of ingestion of a relatively high amount of foods containing sorbitol.
History of having received a surgical replacement of the aortic valve.
A history of exposure during pregnancy (including during the period of conception) of the mother of a fetus or child to methamphetamine.

Mottling (spots or blotches with different shades) of the retinal pigment epithelium in the mother of the index patient. Retinal mottling may indicate a mosaic X-linked inactivation state in the mother.
History of recently intermittent breastfeeding an infant (i.e., on and off).

comments: Recent on and off breastfeeding can be a risk factor for galactocele and breast abscess.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality whose distribution and appearance are well-defined and localized to a specific area.
Applies to an abnormality that displays multiple indentations and/or elevations producing an irregular surface contour.
Applies to an abnormality with irregular, radiating, or spiky edges.

comments: A spiculated mass is a centrally dense lesion seen on mammography with sharp lines radiating from its margin. The spicules can vary greatly in length, from a few millimeters to several centimeters. In malignant lesions, spicules represent a mixture of tumor cells and fibrosis invading the normal tissue surrounding the lesion.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality whose distribution and appearance resembles series of a concentric circles or spirals.
Applies to an abnormality that is located on hair shafts.

nodes.lbl: Prolonged use of tightly fitting footwear

definition.val: History of use of tightly fitting shoes for a long period of time.

comments: This may be a risk factor for tinea pedis.

definition.val: Applies to a sign or symptom that is provoked or brought about ingestion of high quantities of protein.

comments: Metabolic triggers for late-onset urea cycle disorders include excessive protein intake.

definition.val: A tendency to reject a variety of foods and/or to eat a restricted range of foods.
Applied to a sign or symptom that is worsened by exposure to sunlight.
Applied to a sign or symptom that is made worse by exposure to heat (high ambient temperatures).
Applied to a sign or symptom that is made worse by excessive sweating.

comments: High temperatures, high humidity, excessive sweating can exacerbate cutaneous wounds.

definition.val: A past medical history of having long, full hair.
History of exposure to ultraviolet light, such as may be encountered with outdoor activities, living near equator, radiation.
A history of intravaginal cleansing with a liquid solution for personal hygiene or aesthetic reasons.

comments: Douching represents a risk factor for bacterial vaginosis.

nodes.lbl: Exposure to oily lotions or creams

definition.val: A history of applying oily lotions or creams to the skin.

comments: This may be a risk factor for tinea versicolor.

definition.val: A history of exposure to repetitive impact on palm or hand, such as working with a jackhammer.

comments: This history can be a risk factor for carpal tunnel syndrome.

definition.val: Applied to lesions whose distribution is confined or restricted to the palms of the hands and soles of the feet.

nodes.lbl: History of high-pressure injection injury of the hand

definition.val: Medical history of the hand having come into contact with the nozzle of a high-pressure injecting system such as a paint gun or air compressor.

nodes.lbl: History of recent peripheral venous catheter

definition.val: A history of placement of a peripehral venous catheter in the hours, days, or weeks before the current presenting complaint.

nodes.lbl: Occupational history of putting fingers in other people's mouths

definition.val: A history of exposure of fingers by putting them in other peoples' mouths occupationally, e.g., as a dentist or physician.

comments: This is a risk factor for Herpetic whitlow.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality whose distribution and appearance are limited to or predominantly affect a single finger.
Applies to an abnormality whose distribution and appearance affect more than one finger.

nodes.lbl: Pain disproportionate to abnormality on physical exam

definition.val: Exquisite tenderness in a specific region, beyond what one would surmise from examination.

comments: This finding may be observed with several diseases including necrotizing fasciitis and gas gangrene.

nodes.lbl: History of recent participation in athletics

definition.val: Medical history of participation in sports (football, basketball, soccer, skiing) in the hours, days, or weeks prior to the current presenting complaint.
History of trauma months to years ago, including but not limited to fracture or other orthopedic injury at the symptomatic area.

comments: This feature may suggest scaphoid nonunion advanced collapse, scapholunate advanced collapse, or distal radioulnar joint arthritis.

definition.val: A medical history of hving had a spinal cord injury prior to the presenting complaint.

comments: Spinal cord injury (SCI) results from direct or indirect spinal cord damage. The most common SCI cause is acute trauma from motor vehicular crashes (MVCs), although the condition may also arise from insidious etiologies such as malignancies and chronic tuberculous infection. Spinal cord lesions may lead to permanent disability, significant morbidity, and even mortality. High spinal injuries often impair cardiorespiratory function, requiring emergent interventions.

definition.val: Paresthesias in anterior/medial aspect of the thigh (anterior and medial cutaneous nerves of the thigh) and/or medial aspect of the lower leg and foot (saphenous distribution). May be accompanied by weakness in the quadriceps.
History of having been treateed with digoxin.

comments: Digoxin is a cardiac glycoside derived from the foxglove plant (digitalis species). It has inotropic effects and is utilized in the management of systolic dysfunction in patients with congestive heart failure (CHF) and as an atrioventricular nodal blocking agent for managing atrial tachydysrhythmias. Gastrointestinal upset is the most common symptom of digoxin toxicity. Patients also may report visual symptoms, which classically present as a yellow-green discoloration, and cardiovascular symptoms, such as palpitations, dyspnea, and syncope.

nodes.lbl: Pain exacerbated by passive flexion at joint

definition.val: Pain in extremity that worsens with joint is passively flexed.

comments: This feature may be observed in acute compartment syndrome.

definition.val: Pain worsens with wrist ulnar or radial deviation.

comments: Ulnar: Triangular fibrocartilage complex tear; Ulnocarpal abutment syndrome Radial: Triangular fibrocartilage complex tear.

definition.val: A history of exposure to frequent squatting/kneeling and/or heavy lifting.

comments: Occupations that require frequent kneeling such as roofing, construction workers, plumbers, electricians, landscapers, gardeners, carpenters, and housekeepers may increase risk of prepatellar bursitis and knee osteoarthritis.

definition.val: Localization of symptoms such as numbness or paresthesias on the skin over the upper chest, specifically above the clavicle and over the lateral aspect of the neck, corresponding to the distribution of the supraclavicular nerve.

comments: May be observed with clavicle shaft fracture.

definition.val: Applies to the localization of findings corresponding to the distribution of the radial nevrve, such as (i)paresthesias on the dorsoradial aspect of the hand and wrist, including the dorsal thumb, index finger and middle finger and (ii) Weakness in elbow extension, wrist extension, finger extension, thumb extension and/or supination of the forearm.

nodes.lbl: Occupational exposure to frequent hand trauma

definition.val: A history of exposure to repeated hand trauma as observed with occupations in which use of hands is frequent such as construction, food preparation and serving related occupations, and transportation and material moving occupations.

Applies to an abnormality whose distribution is limited to or predominantly affects the fleshy mass on the volar (palmar) aspect of the digit, distal to the distal interphalangeal (DIP) joint (known as the pulp of the finger).

synonyms.val: Symptoms localized to pulp of the finger

nodes.lbl: Snapping sensation at time of injury

definition.val: A popping or snapping sensation (noise or feeling) at the time of an injury.

comments: Sudden snapping or popping at the time of an injury can be a sign of significant damage to muscles, tendons, ligaments, or bones.

definition.val: Pagetoid spread connotes the spread of single neoplastic cells, and small nests of them above the basal layer in a variety of melanocytic and nonmelanocytic neoplasms similar to the pattern seen in Paget's and in extramammary Paget disease.

comments: Pagetoid proliferation of single melanocytic cells or small nests of melanocytes may be seen in a variety of melanocytic neoplasms including pagetoid spitz nevi, de novo epithelioid melanocytic dysplasia, and melanoma. Pagetoid melanocytosis may also be a common feature of acral nevi, traumatized nevi, or in nevi after acute sun exposure.

definition.val: Central scarring of skin lesions.

nodes.lbl: History of thumb sucking past the age of four years

definition.val: Thumb sucking is a behavior that ceases spontaneously in most children between 2 and 4 years of age. If thumb sucking continues, negative consequences can occur, such as a deformity of the nail or paronychia. This term refers to a history of habitual thumb sucking past the age of 4 years of life.
Salter-Harris fractures (physeal fractures) refer to fractures through a growth plate (physis) and are, therefore, specifically applied to bone fractures in children.

A type of physeal (Salter-Harris) fracture in which the fracture line extends through the physis or within the growth plate. Type I fractures are due to the longitudinal force applied through the physis, which splits the epiphysis from the metaphysis.

comments: Beware that a normal radiograph cannot exclude a physis injury in a symptomatic pediatric patient. A radiograph may be normal due to lack of bony involvement, and mild to moderate soft tissue swelling may be noted. Look for the widening of the physis or displacement of the epiphysis, which may suggest a fracture. Diagnosis is based on clinical findings, such as the presence of focal tenderness or swelling surrounding the growth plate.

definition.val: A type of physeal (Salter-Harris) fracture in which the fracture extends through both the physis and metaphysis. These are most common and occur away from the joint space. When the small corner of the metaphysis is visible, this is known as a corner sign or Thurston-Holland fragment.
A type of physeal (Salter-Harris) fracture in which the fracture is intra-articular and extends from the physis into the epiphysis.

comments: If the fracture extends the complete length of the physis, this type of fracture may form two epiphyseal segments. Since the epiphysis is involved, damage to the articular cartilage may occur. One example is a Tillaux fracture of the ankle, which is a fracture of the anterolateral aspect of the growth plate and epiphysis.

definition.val: A type of physeal (Salter-Harris) fracture in which the fracture is intra-articular and passes through the epiphysis, physis, and metaphysis.

comments: As this fracture involves the epiphysis, the articular cartilage may be damaged. Types III and IV fractures each carry a risk for growth retardation, altered joint mechanics, and functional impairment. Therefore, both require urgent orthopedic evaluation.

definition.val: A type of physeal (Salter-Harris) fracture that is due to a crush or compression injury of the growth plate. In type V, the force is transmitted through the epiphysis and physis, potentially disrupting the germinal matrix, hypertrophic region, and vascular supply.

comments: Though Harris-Salter V fractures are very rare, they may be seen in electric shock, frostbite, and irradiation cases. As this fracture pattern tends to result from severe injury, these typically have a poor prognosis leading to bone growth arrest.

definition.val: Recent or remote history of distal radius fracture of the symptomatic arm.

comments: This finding refers to a medical history of distal radius fracture in the arm that is the focus of current medical attention. This finding may relate to scapholunate advanced collapse, distal radioulnar joint arthritis, and scapholunate ligament injury.

nodes.lbl: History of triangular fibrocartilage complex tear

definition.val: Recent or remote history of a tear in the triangular fibrocartilage complex (TFCC). The TFCC is a load-bearing structure between the lunate, triquetrum, and ulnar head. The function of the TFCC is to act as a stabilizer for the ulnar aspect of the wrist. The TFCC is at risk for either acute or chronic degenerative injury.

comments: Such a history is important for the diagnosis of Distal radioulnar joint (DRUJ) arthritis.

definition.val: A descriptor indicating that a condition, lesion, or abnormality is confined to the lower eyelid. This localization may be relevant for various ocular conditions, including infections, structural abnormalities, or dermatological diseases affecting the lower eyelid region.

comments: An anatomical spatial pattern or localization referring to the lower eyelid, the smaller, less mobile fold of skin and tissue positioned below the eye.

definition.val: Applies to an abnormality that is situated in an eyelid.
Paresthesias on the lateral aspect of the shoulder and upper arm. Weakness in shoulder abduction (deltoid) and shoulder external rotation (teres minor).
Refers to pain that is worse when the individual bears weight with the affected body part.

comments: This feature may be observed in conditions such as hip fracture, tibial shaft fracture, calcaneus fracture, and ankle sprain.

definition.val: Applied to a sign or symptom that is worsened by treatment or supplementation with vitamin D.
Applied to a sign or symptom that becomes worse with exposure to sunlight.

comments: This feature represents a clinically observed pattern of symptom worsening in disorders like CYP24A1-related infantile idiopathic hypercalcemia.

definition.val: History of prolonged compressive pressure in the axillary region.

comments: Prolonged compressive pressure in the axillary region is a risk factor for compressive radial mononeuropathy.

definition.val: Applied to a sign or symptom that is worsened by treatment with colchizine.
Distribution of findings in hands and feet with a distal to proximal gradient of severity (being more severe in the more distal locations).

comments: Early peripheral neuropathy may present as sensory alterations that are often progressive, including sensory loss, numbness, pain, or burning sensations in a stocking and glove distribution of the extremities.

definition.val: Applied to a sign or symptom that is worsened by treatment with a pulmonary vasodilator medication such as prostacyclin analogs, endothelin receptor antagonists, and phosphodiesterase-5 (PDE5) inhibitors.
History of exposure to mercury.

nodes.lbl: Pain exacerbated by wrist ulnar deviation

definition.val: Pain worsens with wrist ulnar deviation (i.e., the pain is exacerbated if the hand is moved towards the side with the fifth finger).

nodes.lbl: Pain exacerbated by wrist radial deviation

definition.val: Pain worsens with wrist radial deviation (i.e., the pain is exacerbated if the hand is moved towards the side with the thumb).

nodes.lbl: Transillumination test shows high light transmission

definition.val: A transillumination test involves shining light through an area of the body or a mass. This term refers to a transillumination test that shows especially high (positive) transillumination, which may be observed when a mass or swelling contains clear fluid rather than solid tissue or blood.

comments: Ganglion cysts may show high transillumination.

definition.val: Past medical history of surgical procedure on the eye or its surroundings.
